mnemonics: Zorn Orders Every Chain's Max (Zorn's Lemma: Every partially ordered set in which every chain has an upper bound contains at least one maximal element.)
AC/DC: Axiom of Choice / Dependent Choice (A mnemonic linking the famous band to the Axiom of Choice and the weaker Axiom of Dependent Choice.)
Choose Wisely (The Axiom of Choice lets you 'choose' an element from each set in a collection, even if there's no defined rule for the choice.)
keyPoints: Zorn's Lemma states that if every chain (totally ordered subset) in a partially ordered set has an upper bound, then the set contains at least one maximal element.
The Axiom of Choice asserts that given any collection of non-empty sets, it is possible to choose exactly one element from each set.
These statements are equivalent to the Well-Ordering Principle and are fundamental axioms in set theory with vast applications in analysis, topology, and algebra.
commonMistakes: Assuming Zorn's Lemma constructs a maximum element (it only guarantees a maximal element, which is not necessarily the largest element in the entire set).
Applying the Axiom of Choice to empty sets (it only applies to collections of non-empty sets).
Thinking these principles are 'theorems' that can be proven without assuming them as axioms in the standard Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory framework.
